prose:
**Ethylene frontier-orbital result** — Property: HOMO–LUMO gap from frontier molecular orbitals. Level of theory: DFT tier `standard`, explicitly `b3lyp/def2-tzvp` (B3LYP functional, def2-TZVP basis). Geometry: fixed user-supplied XYZ at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz`; this was a single-point orbital calculation, not a geometry optimization. Environment: gas phase (`solvent: null`). Charge 0, multiplicity 1. Software/backend: PySCF. SCF converged: true, in 6 cycles. Engine integrity trustworthy verdict: true. Provenance: computed.

Headline result: **HOMO–LUMO gap = 7.4357165 eV**.
- HOMO = -7.6155818 eV
- LUMO = -0.17986527 eV
- Total electronic energy = -78.623486 hartree = -2139.4541 eV

Warnings: no warnings were reported by the chemkit result.

Reproducibility: chemkit command reported by the engine was `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --stdout json --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpeoe_c8d4/live.json`. The result JSON path in the invocation is `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpeoe_c8d4/live.json`. The raw chemkit result returned to me did not include a separate live `.out` log path, so I am not inventing one.
